interviewStages: Behavioral: ownership, taste, fast-moving research-engineering culture
codingTopics: NLP and pipelines: tokenization, chunking, retrieval over documents
Streaming: token streams and batched inference
Search and ranking: vector plus keyword retrieval, top-K with heaps
Data structures: tries, inverted indexes, LRU caches
systemDesignTopics: Design a multimodal training pipeline (data ingestion, modality alignment)
Design an inference platform serving multimodal queries
Design a RAG pipeline over a large private document corpus
Design a real-time model-serving system with batching and autoscaling

coding: At Reka AI, given a stream of model inference requests, batch them to maximize GPU utilization while keeping per-request latency under a bound. Discuss the queue and complexity.
Chunk a long document into overlapping passages for retrieval, respecting sentence boundaries. Discuss the sliding-window approach.
Given a query and an inverted index over embeddings, return the top-K most similar items using a heap. Discuss ranking.
Deduplicate near-identical generated outputs using shingling and hashing. Walk through complexity.
Implement an LRU cache for expensive model inference requests keyed by a prompt hash with TTL eviction. Discuss the data structure.
behavioral: Tell me about a project you are proud of at Reka AI scale and the hardest tradeoff you made.
Give me an example that shows ownership, taste, fast-moving research-engineering culture.
Describe a time you disagreed with a teammate or manager. How did it resolve?
Tell me about a time you took ownership of an ambiguous problem end to end.
systemDesign: Design a multimodal training pipeline (data ingestion, modality alignment).
Design an inference platform serving multimodal queries.
Design a RAG pipeline over a large private document corpus.
values: Reka AI's mission carries real responsibility. Describe a time you weighed capability against safety.
How do you evaluate an AI feature where a wrong answer has real consequences?
Tell me about a time you were rigorous about data quality or evaluation when it slowed you down.
other: How does Reka compare to OpenAI or Google DeepMind on multimodal?

Reka AI Interview Guide (2026): Multimodal AI Models

Updated May 6, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Reka AI
reka.ai ↗

Reka AI builds frontier multimodal models — combining text, image, audio, and video reasoning. Founded by ex-Google DeepMind, Meta, and Baidu researchers (Yi Tay and team). Series B in 2024. The interview emphasizes deep ML research engineering, multimodal architectures, and the unique tradeoffs of training cross-modal frontier systems.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding (Python with PyTorch fluency) → onsite virtual: 2 coding/ML, 1 ML system design, 1 research deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Research-track candidates get a paper-discussion round. Cycle: 4–6 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a multimodal training pipeline (data ingestion, modality alignment)

- Design an inference platform serving multimodal queries

- Discuss tradeoffs between encoder-only vs decoder-only multimodal architectures

- Coding: medium-hard DSA, often ML-flavored

- Behavioral: ownership, taste, fast-moving research-engineering culture

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE: $200K–$280K total (Singapore HQ; US offers higher)

- Senior SE / Research Eng: $290K–$400K total

- Staff / Senior Researcher: $410K–$600K+ total at top of band

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Python and PyTorch deeply

- Understand multimodal architectures (vision encoders, cross-attention, late fusion)

- Brush up on distributed training, sequence parallelism, and inference optimization

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Reka remote-friendly?

Hubs in Singapore (HQ) and remote across US/EU/APAC. Many roles distributed.

How does Reka compare to OpenAI or Google DeepMind on multimodal?

Reka punches above its weight on multimodal, particularly for an independent lab. Comp below US-frontier-lab top of band but competitive for Singapore-based ML roles.

What is the engineering culture?

Research-engineering blended; international team across multiple time zones. Strong async culture and high research bar.
